name: University of California, Los Angeles
meets_full_need_notes: UCLA, as a public university, does not guarantee to meet 100% of demonstrated financial need. Average need met is approximately 83% for domestic students. California residents receive significantly better aid via Cal Grant and Blue and Gold Opportunity Plan.
no_loan_notes: UCLA does not have a no-loan policy. Loans are standard components of most aid packages. The Blue and Gold Opportunity Plan covers fees/tuition for California residents with family incomes under $80,000, but does not eliminate loans from packages entirely.

merit_thresholds.notes: UCLA offers some merit-based scholarships through departments, alumni associations, and the Regents Scholarship (highly competitive, renewable). UC Regents Scholarship is the flagship merit award. Many departmental and external scholarships are available.

ed_aid_implications: UCLA does not offer ED or EA. All applicants are reviewed in the regular decision pool.
special_programs: UC Blue and Gold Opportunity Plan (tuition/fees covered for CA residents under $80k income)
Bruin Promise (for entering freshmen with Pell eligibility)
appeal_policy: UCLA students may appeal their financial aid package through the Financial Aid and Scholarships office by submitting a Special Circumstance Review form. Appeals are considered for significant changes in family finances, unusual expenses, or errors.
